skillName: Use formal methods for addition of integers
misconceptionSummary: Students don't understand why carrying works — they see it as an arbitrary rule.

visuals.ruleText: Carrying is regrouping: 10 ones = 1 ten, 10 tens = 1 hundred
visuals.subText: When a column overflows past 9, the excess forms a unit in the next column

steps.narration: Carrying is not a rule to memorise — it is regrouping. Ten ones fill up a ones column completely and form exactly one ten. That ten moves to the tens column. The column can only hold a single digit.

Expand the addition to see what carrying represents. Seven plus six is thirteen — one ten and three ones. That ten joins the eighty from the tens column, giving ninety-three. Carrying is just moving that regrouped ten.
The column method compresses the expanded addition into a compact notation. The carried digit is the regrouped unit — it is the ten from thirteen moving into the tens column.

visuals.label: Carry = regroup: 10 ones become 1 ten in the next column

steps.narration: Ninety-three. The carried digit is a regrouped unit — it is not magic, just place value in action.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: treating carrying as an arbitrary step to memorise. Carrying is regrouping — 10 ones become 1 ten. It follows directly from how place value works.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: Carrying is just regrouping. When ones reach ten, they form a single ten that moves to the tens column. Place value explains every step.
